files.content: This file — see root object for full artifact.

action_items: Publish SAGE's regulated-industry compliance story as a differentiation pillar in all external messaging
Build a dedicated 'Compliance Mode' landing page targeting ISO 13485 / IEC 62304 / DO-178C buyers
Create a comparison teardown (SAGE vs CrewAI vs AutoGen) focused on audit trail depth and HITL gates
Partner with 1-2 regulatory consultancies (medtech/avionics) to co-market the whitespace position
Add a 'Compliance Pack' to the skill marketplace (public visibility) as a proof point

axis_x.label: Human Control Level
axis_x.rationale: Core buying criterion in regulated industries. Distinguishes tools safe for compliance-gated workflows from tools that assume full autonomy.

axis_y.label: Domain Specialization
axis_y.top_pole: Domain-Specialized (firmware, PCB, medtech, avionics — purpose-built runners)
axis_y.rationale: Depth of domain tooling (compilers, DRC, SPICE, Gerbers) determines whether a framework can actually execute in vertical industries vs just orchestrate LLM calls.

quadrants.top_left: Domain-Specialized + Autonomous — HIGH RISK ZONE (regulated industries cannot operate here)
quadrants.top_right: Domain-Specialized + HITL — SAGE's WHITESPACE (compliance-safe vertical AI)
quadrants.bottom_left: General Purpose + Autonomous — COMMODITY ZONE (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI)
quadrants.bottom_right: General Purpose + HITL — ENTERPRISE WORKFLOW ZONE (Vertex AI, Flowise)

competitor_profiles.name: LangGraph
competitor_profiles.value_proposition: Stateful, cyclical multi-agent orchestration for developers building complex LLM workflows. Graph-based state machine with first-class streaming and checkpointing.

pricing.model: Open-source (MIT). LangSmith observability platform: Free tier + $39/mo/seat Pro.
pricing.enterprise: LangSmith Enterprise — custom pricing, SSO, dedicated support.

key_differentiators: Most mature LLM orchestration graph primitives in the OSS ecosystem
First-class interrupt/resume for human-in-the-loop (but opt-in, not mandatory)
Deep LangChain ecosystem integration (tools, retrievers, memory)
Streaming and real-time agent state inspection via LangSmith
weaknesses: No domain-specific execution runners (firmware, PCB, ML pipelines)
HITL is optional and unenforced — not suitable for regulated compliance
No audit log as compliance record — observability is for debugging, not regulation
Steep learning curve; graph wiring is verbose for non-developers
competitor_profiles.target_customer: Python developers building custom LLM-powered applications

competitor_profiles.name: AutoGen
competitor_profiles.value_proposition: Conversational multi-agent framework where agents collaborate through structured dialogue. Strong at code generation with sandboxed Docker execution.

pricing.enterprise: No direct enterprise offering; consumed via Azure AI Studio (pay-per-token).

key_differentiators: Conversational agent topology — agents negotiate task completion through dialogue
Built-in Docker sandboxed code execution
Strong Microsoft/Azure ecosystem integration
AutoGen Studio: low-code visual agent builder (beta)
weaknesses: No mandatory HITL — approval gates are developer-implemented, not enforced
No domain-specific runners (firmware toolchains, EDA, SPICE simulation)
No audit log as compliance record
Agent conversations can become non-deterministic and hard to audit in regulated contexts
Primarily code-generation focused; weak for non-SWE domains
competitor_profiles.target_customer: Research teams and enterprises building code-centric multi-agent systems on Azure

competitor_profiles.name: CrewAI
competitor_profiles.value_proposition: Role-based multi-agent crews with defined agents, tasks, and tools. Simple API for assembling autonomous agent teams without deep LLM framework knowledge.

pricing.model: Open-source core (MIT). CrewAI+ cloud platform: Free tier (limited runs) + $99/mo Pro.
pricing.enterprise: Enterprise plan — custom pricing, SLA, on-prem deployment.

key_differentiators: Simplest API for role-based agent teams — fastest time-to-demo
Large community and growing skill/tool marketplace
CrewAI+ managed cloud reduces infra burden
Strong ecosystem of pre-built agent templates
weaknesses: Fully autonomous by design — no mandatory HITL approval gates
No domain-specific execution environment (just LLM + generic tools)
No immutable audit log or compliance record
Skill depth is shallow vs domain-specialized runners
Template-driven approach limits adaptability for complex regulated workflows
competitor_profiles.target_customer: Non-technical teams and startups wanting quick multi-agent automation without coding depth
competitor_profiles.matrix_position: bottom_left, trending toward bottom_right — General Purpose + Mostly Autonomous

competitor_profiles.name: Vertex AI Agent Builder
competitor_profiles.value_proposition: Managed enterprise AI agent platform on Google Cloud. Drag-and-drop agent construction with Gemini models, built-in grounding, and enterprise IAM.

pricing.model: Pay-per-use (per 1K chars input/output). No free tier for production.
pricing.enterprise: Enterprise Agreements via Google Cloud sales — committed use discounts available.

key_differentiators: Fully managed — no infra to run or scale
Native Google Search and Vertex AI Search grounding
Enterprise-grade IAM, VPC-SC, CMEK for regulated data
Multi-modal agents (text, image, code) via Gemini
weaknesses: Vendor lock-in to Google Cloud; high switching cost
No domain-specific runners for firmware, PCB, simulation
HITL is available but not enforced as a compliance requirement
No open-source audit log — compliance records tied to GCP logging (external dependency)
High cost at scale; not accessible for startups or low-budget teams
competitor_profiles.target_customer: Enterprises already on GCP wanting managed AI agents without MLOps overhead
competitor_profiles.matrix_position: bottom_right — General Purpose + HITL available (not enforced)

competitor_profiles.name: Flowise
competitor_profiles.value_proposition: Visual drag-and-drop builder for LLM flows and agent pipelines. Low-code, self-hostable, with 100+ pre-built integrations.

pricing.model: Open-source (Apache 2.0). Flowise Cloud: Free tier + $35/mo Starter + $99/mo Pro.

key_differentiators: Most accessible entry point — non-developers can build agents visually
100+ pre-built nodes (LLMs, vector stores, tools, APIs)
Self-hostable with Docker in minutes
Strong community adoption; fastest-growing visual LLM builder
weaknesses: Visual flows break down for complex logic; not suitable for regulated multi-step agent loops
No mandatory HITL enforcement — approval is a manual node, not a system guarantee
No domain-specific runners; purely orchestration-layer tooling
No compliance audit log — audit trail is a feature gap
Scalability limits for enterprise production workloads
competitor_profiles.target_customer: Citizen developers, SMBs, and prototypers wanting visual LLM app construction
competitor_profiles.matrix_position: bottom_right — General Purpose + Semi-HITL (visual, not enforced)

whitespace_analysis.description: No competitor occupies the intersection of (a) deep domain execution runners (firmware cross-compilation, PCB DRC/ERC, SPICE simulation, ML pipelines) with (b) mandatory, auditable, compliance-grade human approval gates. Every competitor either trades off domain depth for generality, or trades off HITL enforcement for autonomy speed.

target_segments: Medical device manufacturers under ISO 13485 / IEC 62304 / FDA 21 CFR Part 11
Avionics software teams under DO-178C / DO-254
Automotive embedded teams under ISO 26262 / AUTOSAR
opportunity_size.pain_point: Regulated teams cannot use autonomous AI agents without risking audit failure. Existing frameworks offer HITL as an opt-in feature, not a system guarantee. SAGE makes HITL structurally mandatory — agents cannot bypass the approval gate, the audit log is immutable, and every decision is traced end-to-end.
opportunity_size.urgency_driver: Regulatory bodies (FDA, EASA, TÜV) are beginning to ask how AI was used in design and verification processes. Teams that cannot produce an AI audit trail are increasingly at risk of extended review cycles or rejection.

sage_unique_claims: HITL is architecturally enforced, not optional — agent proposals cannot execute without human sign-off
Immutable per-solution audit log (SQLite) is the compliance record, not a debugging tool
Domain runners (OpenFW, OpenEDA, OpenSim, OpenML) speak the toolchain language of regulated industries — not just LLM calls
Compounding intelligence via vector memory means the system gets better without retraining — zero additional compliance risk from model updates
YAML-first declarative manifest makes agent configurations version-controlled, reviewable, and auditable by non-developers (including regulators)
Open-source core + private solution isolation: regulated IP never touches a third-party server

strategic_implications.implication: Own the 'Compliance-Safe AI' category before a well-funded competitor spots the whitespace
actions: Publish a SAGE Compliance Whitepaper covering ISO 13485, IEC 62304, DO-178C, ISO 26262 alignment
Build a 'Compliance Mode' preset in project.yaml that enforces stricter HITL gates and extends audit log retention
Get SAGE cited in at least one published regulated-industry case study within 6 months

strategic_implications.implication: LangGraph is the most technically capable competitor and could extend into regulated verticals
actions: Monitor LangGraph's HITL roadmap — if they add mandatory approval enforcement, SAGE's moat narrows
Differentiate on domain runners (firmware, PCB, simulation) which LangGraph has no path to build
Offer a LangGraph migration guide: teams already using LangGraph can wrap their workflows in SAGE's HITL + audit layer

strategic_implications.implication: CrewAI is fastest-growing in the non-technical buyer segment and will expand upmarket
actions: SAGE should not compete on simplicity against CrewAI — compete on depth, compliance, and compounding intelligence
Publish a clear 'When CrewAI is wrong for you' guide targeting regulated-industry engineering leads
Position Agent Gym + Glicko-2 ratings as enterprise-grade proof of agent quality improvement — a capability CrewAI has no equivalent for

strategic_implications.implication: Vertex AI is the enterprise default for GCP shops — SAGE must be the on-prem / air-gapped alternative
actions: Emphasize zero-cloud-dependency: SAGE runs fully offline with Ollama — critical for defense and aerospace
Build a one-page TCO comparison: Vertex AI token costs vs SAGE self-hosted with local LLM
Target GCP shops already paying Vertex AI fees with a 'bring your own infra' SAGE migration path

strategic_implications.implication: The skill marketplace is an untapped moat — private skills = proprietary IP, not just configuration
actions: Launch a 'Certified Skills' program: third-party vendors publish regulated-domain skills (e.g., a TÜV-verified safety analysis skill)
Price private skill hosting as a future SaaS revenue line
Recruit 3 domain experts (one medtech, one avionics, one automotive) to contribute public skills as proof of depth
